verses.text: 'Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises,' the God who builds Jerusalem and gathers the outcasts, who 'healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.' He is tender to the hurting. The God of the stars stoops to mend a broken heart.

'Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises,' the God who builds Jerusalem and gathers the outcasts, who 'healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.' He is tender to the hurting. The God of the stars stoops to mend a broken heart.

'Praise ye the LORD: for it is good to sing praises,' the God who builds Jerusalem and gathers the outcasts, who 'healeth the broken in heart, and bindeth up their wounds.' He is tender to the hurting. The God of the stars stoops to mend a broken heart.

He numbers and names the stars, His understanding infinite; He lifts the meek and casts the wicked down. His power and care are joined. The God who orders the heavens also raises the humble of the earth.

He numbers and names the stars, His understanding infinite; He lifts the meek and casts the wicked down. His power and care are joined. The God who orders the heavens also raises the humble of the earth.

He numbers and names the stars, His understanding infinite; He lifts the meek and casts the wicked down. His power and care are joined. The God who orders the heavens also raises the humble of the earth.

Sing to Him with thanksgiving, who covers the sky with clouds, sends rain, grows grass, and gives food to beast and raven. He provides for all. The God of the harvest feeds even the crying young birds.

Sing to Him with thanksgiving, who covers the sky with clouds, sends rain, grows grass, and gives food to beast and raven. He provides for all. The God of the harvest feeds even the crying young birds.

Sing to Him with thanksgiving, who covers the sky with clouds, sends rain, grows grass, and gives food to beast and raven. He provides for all. The God of the harvest feeds even the crying young birds.

He delights not in the horse's strength or man's legs, but 'taketh pleasure in them that fear him,' who hope in His mercy. He values faith over force. What pleases God is not power but a reverent, hoping heart.

He delights not in the horse's strength or man's legs, but 'taketh pleasure in them that fear him,' who hope in His mercy. He values faith over force. What pleases God is not power but a reverent, hoping heart.

Zion is called to praise her God, who strengthens her gates, blesses her children, makes peace in her borders, and fills her with the finest wheat. He guards and provides for His city. Peace and plenty are the gifts of God to His people.

Zion is called to praise her God, who strengthens her gates, blesses her children, makes peace in her borders, and fills her with the finest wheat. He guards and provides for His city. Peace and plenty are the gifts of God to His people.

He sends His word to the earth, gives snow like wool and frost like ashes, then sends His word again and melts them. Nature obeys His command. The same word that freezes and thaws governs all the seasons.

He sends His word to the earth, gives snow like wool and frost like ashes, then sends His word again and melts them. Nature obeys His command. The same word that freezes and thaws governs all the seasons.

He sends His word to the earth, gives snow like wool and frost like ashes, then sends His word again and melts them. Nature obeys His command. The same word that freezes and thaws governs all the seasons.

He sends His word to the earth, gives snow like wool and frost like ashes, then sends His word again and melts them. Nature obeys His command. The same word that freezes and thaws governs all the seasons.

He shows His word and statutes to Jacob, dealing so 'with no nation,' who have not known His judgments. Israel's privilege was His revealed word. To possess God's word is the greatest of all blessings.
